head: 90eb694

primitive_census.method: grep PrimitiveKind:: across all crates (src + tests)
primitive_census.VALIDATE_gate_notes: Zero occurrences in Rust source or TypeScript bindings. Three categories of appearances exist and all are legitimate: (1) docs/ENGINE-SPEC.md, docs/GLOSSARY.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/DSL-SPECIFICATION.md use VALIDATE/GATE in historical context with explicit 2026-04-14 retirement banners; (2) docs/validation/paper-prototype-handlers.md uses the original 12 set but carries the required caution banner; (3) node_modules/chevrotain and node_modules/langium use the word GATE as a parser predicate concept, entirely unrelated to the Benten primitive vocabulary.

phase_1_executable_verification.claim: Exactly 8 primitives are executable in Phase 1
phase_1_executable_verification.test_coverage: primitive_types.rs::phase_1_executable_subset_is_eight_primitives uses set-equality assertion (not count+containment) — R4 triage m2 fix verified

phase_2_stub_dispatch_verification.claim: WAIT, STREAM, SUBSCRIBE, SANDBOX return EvalError::PrimitiveNotImplemented at call time
phase_2_stub_dispatch_verification.verification_method: direct dispatch arm read, NOT trust of is_phase_1_executable()
phase_2_stub_dispatch_verification.separate_module_files: None — no wait.rs, stream.rs, subscribe.rs, sandbox.rs exist under primitives/. The dispatch arm in mod.rs IS the implementation. Confirmed via glob.
phase_2_stub_dispatch_verification.test_coverage: primitive_types.rs::phase_two_primitives_return_not_implemented_at_call_time iterates all four kinds and asserts Err(PrimitiveNotImplemented(k)) with pattern matching on the inner kind value

cap_refresh_verification.claim: Step 5c added CALL-entry and ITERATE batch-boundary cap-refresh via PrimitiveHost::check_capability
cap_refresh_verification.iterate_mechanism: Entry refresh fires unconditionally at iter 0 (line 94); per-batch loop at `boundary`, `2*boundary`, etc. (lines 104-114). Uses `host.iterate_batch_boundary()` for cadence. Scope pulled from `requires` property, falls back to literal string 'iterate'.
cap_refresh_verification.call_mechanism: Entry refresh fires before attenuation check, timeout check, or handler dispatch. Scope prefers `child_scope`, falls back to `requires`, then to literal 'call'.
cap_refresh_verification.test_coverage: crates/benten-eval/tests/cap_refresh_toctou.rs — 6 targeted tests using RevokeAfterNHost synthetic PrimitiveHost. Tests cover: (a) CALL-entry denial routes ON_DENIED; (b) CALL-entry permit proceeds; (c) ITERATE multi-batch denial at correct boundary; (d) ITERATE single-batch no spurious refresh; (e) ITERATE entry-denial; (f) ITERATE custom boundary honored.

new_primitive_hunt.method: grep for all pub enum across crates; inspect each

candidates_inspected.name: Expr / BinaryOp / UnaryOp
candidates_inspected.verdict: TRANSFORM expression AST nodes — not primitive variants

candidates_inspected.name: BatchOp / PendingOp (benten-graph)
candidates_inspected.verdict: storage-layer operation shapes (put/delete), not primitive kinds

candidates_inspected.name: ChangeKind
candidates_inspected.verdict: change-stream event discriminant, not a primitive

candidates_inspected.name: PendingOp (benten-caps)
candidates_inspected.verdict: capability write-context shape (PutNode/PutEdge/DeleteNode/DeleteEdge), not primitive kinds

candidates_inspected.name: PendingHostOp (benten-engine)
candidates_inspected.verdict: buffered-replay shape for the two-phase write — not a primitive kind

candidates_inspected.name: ViewState / ViewResult / ViewError
candidates_inspected.verdict: IVM view state machine shapes, not primitives

sandbox_scrutiny.assessment: SANDBOX is a type stub only. No WASM execution, no wasmtime calls, no fuel metering code exists. The stub is correctly placed as Phase-2 scope. No SANDBOX usage is performing computation that could be composed from existing primitives — the executor arm correctly rejects at call time with PrimitiveNotImplemented.
sandbox_scrutiny.decomposition_check: N/A — no SANDBOX computation is occurring

classification_presence.notes: WRITE and RESPOND classified deterministic per the semantic used: 'output-to-caller is a pure function of inputs; side effects are separately tracked'. This matches ENGINE-SPEC §5 'deterministic-with-side-effects' framing documented in the is_deterministic() docstring. EMIT is non-deterministic because it couples the handler to observer side effects that the engine cannot replay.

enforcement.mechanism: When sn.deterministic == true, iterates all nodes and fires InvariantViolation::Determinism if any node's kind.is_deterministic() is false
enforcement.gap_noted: validate_subgraph (the finalized-Subgraph path) skips Invariant 9 because the Subgraph struct does not carry the deterministic flag through serialization. Documented with TODO(phase-2) at invariants.rs:137-149 referencing mini-review findings g6-cag-4/g6-opl-5. This is a known pre-existing Phase-2 item, not a new finding.
enforcement.test_coverage: invariants_9_10_12.rs: det_context_rejects_sandbox test verifies SANDBOX in deterministic context fails; primitive_types.rs::deterministic_primitives_match_spec and non_deterministic_primitives_match_spec assert all 12 classifications

determinism_check.context_enforcement: Non-deterministic ops (EMIT, WAIT, SANDBOX, SUBSCRIBE, STREAM) cannot appear in a builder-path subgraph declared deterministic:true without registration-time rejection

pass_1_dispositions.note: The file r6-operation-primitive-linter.json did not exist prior to this audit — the prompt references a 'pass-1' that produced a PASS verdict, but no JSON was written at that time. The present audit is therefore the first written record. The pass-1 verbal verdict (PASS, 2 majors / 4 minors) referred to in the task prompt was about the Step 5b/5c cycle addressing CALL ON_LIMIT error edges and determinism semantics documentation. Those items are verified as resolved in this pass.

call_on_limit_fix.finding: CALL ON_LIMIT edge was undeclared in PrimitiveKind::error_edges()
call_on_limit_fix.status: RESOLVED — PrimitiveKind::Call.error_edges() now returns ['ON_DENIED', 'ON_LIMIT', 'ON_ERROR'] at lib.rs:410; test call_primitive_error_edges_include_on_limit_denied_error pins this; and call::execute routes ON_LIMIT when elapsed > timeout at call.rs:97-107

determinism_semantics_doc.finding: Determinism classification semantics were ambiguous — WRITE/RESPOND classified true was unexplained
determinism_semantics_doc.status: RESOLVED — is_deterministic() docstring (lib.rs:361-394) now explicitly states 'output-to-caller is a pure function of inputs' semantic and names the ENGINE-SPEC §5 'deterministic-with-side-effects' framing. Separate replay-safety classification deferred to Phase 2 with explicit note.

findings.claim: Invariant 9 (determinism) is silently skipped on the validate_subgraph path (finalized Subgraph, not builder snapshot) because the Subgraph struct does not carry the deterministic flag through serialization.
findings.evidence: The comment at invariants.rs:137-149 reads 'Invariant 9 is silently skipped on the finalized Subgraph path because the struct does not carry the deterministic flag through serialization. Phase 2 adds the flag to the DAG-CBOR schema.' This means a round-tripped subgraph loaded via Subgraph::load_verified cannot be re-validated for determinism until Phase 2.
findings.impact: A subgraph originally declared deterministic:true can be re-loaded and validated without its determinism context being checked. In Phase 1 this is bounded: load_verified returns an empty Subgraph stub (see the load_verified TODO comment), so re-validation of a loaded subgraph is a no-op structurally anyway. The risk materialises in Phase 2 when load_verified decodes fully.
findings.fix: Pre-existing known gap, tracked with TODO(phase-2) references to mini-review findings g6-cag-4/g6-opl-5. No action needed in Phase 1. Phase 2 must add the deterministic field to the DAG-CBOR Subgraph schema.

findings.claim: The CALL executor's host dispatch path (via host.call_handler) only fires when BOTH 'target' and 'call_op' properties are present on the OperationNode. Callers that set only 'target' without 'call_op' silently fall through to the legacy no-target happy path returning Value::Null.
findings.evidence: call.rs:112-113: 'if let (Some(Value::Text(target)), Some(Value::Text(callee_op))) = (op.properties.get("target"), op.properties.get("call_op"))' — both required.
findings.impact: In Phase 1 this is by design: call_op is the op discriminant (create/list/get/update/delete) needed by Engine::dispatch_call. A CALL node with only 'target' silently no-ops rather than erroring, which could hide misconfigured subgraphs. The Phase-1 test suite uses the fixture convention of staging both properties when testing the dispatch path (verified in cap_refresh_toctou.rs:125-127).
findings.fix: No Phase-1 action required. Phase 2 should add a registration-time check: a CALL node with 'target' but no 'call_op' should produce a RegistrationError::MissingCallOp rather than silently falling through at runtime.

findings.claim: The evaluator's entry-node detection ('first node without an incoming edge') silently picks the first such node when multiple roots exist. Multi-root subgraphs are not rejected at registration time.
findings.evidence: evaluator.rs:139-156 includes 'TODO(R4b / G7): when engine registration lands, validate that exactly one node has no incoming edges so multi-root handlers are rejected at registration instead of silently picking the first-constructed root.' This is a known gap with a tracked TODO.
findings.impact: A subgraph accidentally constructed with two roots (no incoming edge) will silently execute only from whichever root appears first in the nodes Vec. The second root is unreachable. Phase 1 registration-time checks do not catch this because cycle detection and depth checks traverse from all nodes.
findings.fix: Pre-existing known gap tracked with TODO(R4b/G7). No Phase-1 action. Phase 2 registration must enforce exactly-one-root at registration time.

summary: The 12-primitive vocabulary is correctly implemented, typed, and enforced throughout the codebase. PrimitiveKind has exactly 12 variants matching the 2026-04-14 canonical set. The 8 Phase-1 executable primitives (READ, WRITE, TRANSFORM, BRANCH, ITERATE, CALL, RESPOND, EMIT) are confirmed present with actual executor files. The 4 Phase-2 stubs (WAIT, STREAM, SUBSCRIBE, SANDBOX) are confirmed to return EvalError::PrimitiveNotImplemented via direct dispatch-arm inspection — not inferred from is_phase_1_executable(). Step 5c's cap-refresh additions are confirmed live in iterate.rs (entry refresh + per-batch loop) and call.rs (entry refresh before attenuation), with 6 targeted regression tests exercising denial and permit paths. Zero VALIDATE or GATE primitive references exist in production code or bindings. Zero undocumented primitive-shaped enum types were found. Zero SANDBOX computations exist — the type is a stub only. Determinism classification is present on all 12 variants and enforced at builder-path registration time. Three pre-existing Phase-2 scope items noted as info (Invariant 9 finalized-path gap, CALL missing call_op silent passthrough, multi-root entry detection) — none are new findings introduced by Step 5c.
